changes.evidence: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md line 4 — risk_level: HIGH. Correct: all three defects touch backend/core/pipeline/orchestrator.py and backend/core/scoring/. All are explicit HIGH-risk paths per SOP §3 (Intelligence Core) and §10.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md line 6 — change_type: BEHAVIOUR. Correct: all three defects change scoring output and biomarker interpretation text for affected markers. Analytical output (scores, statuses, interpretation) changes by design.

File exists and read. Defect 1 drop points confirmed at lines 558-566 (score_biomarkers dict comprehension) and 724-731 (generate_insights dict comprehension): both omit unscored_reason from BiomarkerScore serialization. Consumer at line 1868: biomarker_score.get('unscored_reason', '') always returns '' because key is absent. _has_valid_numeric_bounds at lines 1089-1096 delegates to primitives.has_valid_numeric_lab_range — correctly handles one-sided bounds.

File exists and read. UNSCORED_REASON sentinel at lines 39-44 (policy-governed). calculate_biomarker_score at lines 186-234: one-sided handling at lines 212-219 — calls position_in_one_sided_lab_range; returns scored result when pos is not None. Engine-level scoring path is correctly wired for one-sided ranges.

File exists and read. position_in_one_sided_lab_range at lines 42-63: returns 0.55 for in-range (max-only or min-only), 1.02 or 0.02 for out-of-range. has_valid_numeric_lab_range at lines 66-72: returns True when either bound is numeric. Both functions are one-sided compatible.

backend/core/scoring/engine.py line 35: unscored_reason: Optional[str] = None. Set at engine.py:213 when calculate_biomarker_score returns an unscored_reason. The field exists on the dataclass but is NOT included in either dict comprehension at orchestrator.py:558-566 or 724-731.

changes.result: ADVISORY — no repo file; not a blocking gap
changes.evidence: No committed file for this live analysis ID found in repo. This is a runtime artifact, not a repo file path. SOP Stage 2A verifies file paths; this reference is not a file path. Defect specifications are self-contained in the prompt. Corroborating repo evidence: automation_bus/ab_panel_range_drop_investigation.md (read) confirms hdl_cholesterol, triglycerides, hba1c range-drop and unit semantics issues (lines 37-46, 87-92, 112-124).

unscored_reason sentinel defined once at scoring_policy.yaml:16, loaded once at rules.py:39-44. BiomarkerScore dataclass defined once at engine.py:28-35. position_in_one_sided_lab_range defined once at primitives.py:42-63. No duplicate scoring policy authority detected.

Three bounded defects with explicit required behaviour per defect. Out-of-scope list at prompt lines 158-165 explicitly excludes remnant_cholesterol policy, free_testosterone_pct policy, broad scoring redesign, broad unit redesign. Design Rule 5: 'If additional improvements are discovered, report them separately.'

Defect 1: marker with known non-empty unscored_reason must preserve cause into biomarker output path. Defect 2: representative min-only and max-only live-style markers (hdl_cholesterol, triglycerides, active_b12, egfr, folate) must produce scored output. Defect 3: HbA1c value/range unit mismatch must not surface as 'insufficient numeric bounds' — must show truthful reason or valid score.

RISK-1: Adding unscored_reason to biomarker score dicts at orchestrator.py:558-566 and 724-731 propagates new key into build_insight_graph_v1 and other consumers — verify no structural break; STOP 1 applies if broader contract redesign needed. RISK-2: Defect 2 fix changes score values for named markers from 0.0 to non-zero; overall_score and system scores shift — intended, but regression fixtures must use live-style markers (hdl_cholesterol etc.), not synthetic markers. RISK-3: HbA1c unit-mismatch detection must be narrowly gated; must not touch other markers' unit paths; STOP 3 applies if backend/core/units/registry.py requires broader change.

All files in scope (orchestrator.py, scoring/rules.py, analytics/primitives.py, scoring/engine.py) are under backend/core/ — explicit Intelligence Core per SOP §3. HIGH risk is correctly declared. BEHAVIOUR change_type is correct: scoring output changes for affected markers.

5 STOP conditions at prompt lines 222-228. STOP 1: broader contract redesign (maps to RISK-1). STOP 2: upstream data corruption (not triggered — normalize.py correctly preserves one-sided ranges at lines 92-126). STOP 3: larger unit redesign for HbA1c (maps to RISK-3). STOP 4: file scope expansion beyond scoring/orchestrator/DTO. STOP 5: policy decisions required (maps to Defect 3 edge cases). All conditions are binding and proportionate.
run_work_package.py, golden_gate_local.py, update_cursor_status.py not referenced. No tooling-leakage risk identified.

Current branch: fix/integration-stability-backend. Required: fix/unscored-marker-trust-gaps. New branch must be created/checked out before Stage 3 (run_work_package.py start). Cursor responsibility per SOP §0.

advisory_notes: Live analysis 6f702428-ec3e-4e00-9416-280904e9d4b3 is not a committed file; Cursor must either reproduce the diagnosis via run_golden_panel or accept the defect specs as written in the prompt. The ab_panel_range_drop_investigation.md corroborates the defect areas.

Defect 1 fix is targeted: add 'unscored_reason': score.unscored_reason to two dict comprehensions at orchestrator.py:558-566 and 724-731. Verify InsightGraph builder does not break on the new key before claiming Defect 1 complete.

Defect 2 root cause not fully confirmed at hardening time — Cursor must trace why specific markers (active_b12, egfr, folate in particular) fail despite the one-sided range logic being nominally correct in scoring/engine. The second normalizer call at orchestrator.py:542 (from scalar-only simple_biomarkers) does not carry reference ranges; input_reference_ranges dict must be verified to contain the live marker names under the correct canonical keys.

Defect 3: hba1c_layer_b_arbitration.py exists at backend/core/canonical/hba1c_layer_b_arbitration.py — Cursor should check whether this arbitration path is relevant to the unit mismatch detection, or whether the fix belongs in the scoring/DTO path.
